content:
# Recommendations — next plan iter (after iter-092)

## State: GREEN, 7 sorries (was RED@9). Route CONVERGING.
`lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjInverse` EXIT 0 (8565 jobs). Migration route nearly closed:
DualInverse 0, TensorObjSubstrate 1 (dead dup), TensorObjInverse 7.

## TOP PRIORITY — open a ROOT-RECOVERY lane (NEW; unblocks BOTH K1 and B1)
**K1 `pullbackTensorMap_isIso_of_isOpenImmersion` (L807) and B1 `tensorObj_restrict_iso_eq_pullbackTensorMap`
(L837) share ONE blocker: the dropped ROOT lemma `pushforward_mu_appIso_collapse`.**
- It exists at `117100c4:SubProjects/.../AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean:4678` (the
  δ-conjugation EQUATION). The v4.31-migrated working-tree ROOT DROPPED it (+ `deltaConjOfMuComparison`,
  `isIso_oplaxδ_of_conj`), keeping only `isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta` (an `IsIso`
  WITNESS, not the equation).
- **Action:** a `prove`/`refactor` lane on ROOT `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` that re-ports
  `pushforward_mu_appIso_collapse` (+ the 2 siblings if K1's proof needs them) from `117100c4`, applying
  the 9-axis v4.31 pattern. This is upstream — serialize before any K1/B1 work on TensorObjInverse.
- **Then** K1 ports from `117100c4:TensorObjSubstrate.lean:4790` and B1 from the L817-836 skeleton already
  in-file. CAUTION (unchanged): K1's elaboration is 6.4M-HB and OOMed the 2GB host in-place (iter-091) →
  still do the **file-split** for K1, but only AFTER ROOT re-exposes the equation (the split alone does
  nothing while the lemma is missing). The standing file-split directive remains, now correctly sequenced
  *after* ROOT-recovery.

## SECOND — light-sweep `tensorObj_restrict_iso_restrict_compat` (L982), INDEPENDENT of the ROOT gap
The one remaining LIGHT recoverable sorry that does NOT depend on `pushforward_mu_appIso_collapse`. Port
from `117100c4` by name + 9-axis (restriction-naturality compat). Good cheap next-iter win; can co-run
with the ROOT-recovery lane (different files — no build race if ROOT is the only churn upstream).

## DOC FIX (cheap, prover/refactor) — stale "CLOSED" docstring contradicts the sorry
`tensorObj_restrict_iso_eq_pullbackTensorMap` docstring L800 still reads
`**STATUS (iter-051): CLOSED at this level.**` while the body is `sorry` (WALLED iter-092). The body-level
WALLED comment (L817-836) is accurate; the docstring header was not updated. A reader skimming docstrings
would conclude this proof is done. Have the prover/refactor reconcile L800 when it next touches B1.
(lean-auditor `iter092` must-fix; it is the file's only genuine doc-vs-code contradiction.)

## DO NOT RE-ASSIGN (deferred — explicit)
- **Dual-unit FLANK** `dual_unit_iso_restrict_assemble` (L1511) + `dualUnitIso_dualIsoOfIso` (L2315) —
  GENUINE-OPEN, ride the ∞-source presheaf pullback-unit comparison `p` (no informal proof). Needs
  dag-walker / blueprint-writer on the ∞-source BEFORE any prover. Separate future phase. Honest blocker
  comments already in file (L1479-1510).
- **Telescope** `trivialisation_restrict_compat` (L2298) + **terminal** `exists_tensorObj_inverse` (L2372)
  — gated on B1 + the flank; cannot close until upstream sorries clear. Do not attempt until those land.
- **Transitively-hollow helpers** (`tensorObj_unit_iso_restrict_compat(_inner)`,
  `dual_unit_iso_restrict_compat`, `tensorObj_unit_self_duality_collapse`): no fix needed — they are
  pre-placed scaffolding whose consumers are also sorried; the hollow-ness resolves when B1/flank close.

## REUSABLE PATTERNS (recorded in PROJECT_STATUS KB)
- **axis-9 extends to `right_triangle_components`**: `simpa only [L] using (...).right_triangle_components`
  fails on the unit-object `(forget ⋙ restrictScalars 𝟙).obj (unit) ≡ 𝟙_` spelling at reducible
  transparency → `have h := ...; simp only [L] at h; exact h`.
- **Migration recovery requires a TRANSITIVE source check**: a working-tree decl may be sorried not because
  it's hard but because a *different* file's v4.31 migration DROPPED a lemma it depended on. Before
  classifying a sorry as "in-file port", grep the v4.30 proof's `rw`/`exact` targets against the current
  working tree (B1/K1 = `pushforward_mu_appIso_collapse`).

## Coverage debt (deferred again — same rationale)
`archon dag-query unmatched` = 153 (unchanged). No NEW unmatched decls this iter — the prover ported bodies
into already-existing frozen signatures (no new helpers). The engine is still mid-migration; coverage-debt
clearance stays scheduled for the post-recovery cleanup phase (private-mark the genuine helpers, blueprint
the rest). Listing all 153 here is wasteful while the helper set is unstable.

## Blueprint (minor, non-blocking)
- `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` `lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_image_collapse` L9131: `% NOTE` added this
  review for the display-equation hom/inv slip. A blueprint-writer could fix the one-char paraphrase
  (μ.hom → μ.inv in the solved-for-unitor display) next time the chapter is touched; not urgent.
